meta.title: 35-Domain Democratic Renewal Framework — Dependency Graph
meta.description: Machine-readable specification of all framework domains with dependency relationships, theme clusters, urgency ratings, and production status. Companion to domain-network-spec.md.

urgency_legend.critical-2026: Advocacy window closes before or during 2026 midterms
urgency_legend.research-only: Longer-horizon reform; no near-term legislative window

status_legend.scope-doc: Outline and research framework complete; full research not yet executed
status_legend.proposal-only: Covered in democratic-renewal-proposal.md body; no standalone domain file in /domains/

edge_type_legend.prerequisite: Read source domain before target domain; logical dependency
edge_type_legend.cross-reference: Bidirectional; domains reference each other's evidence
edge_type_legend.structural: Target domain provides enforcement mechanism or reform vehicle for source domain

domains.title: Electoral Reform / Voting Rights and Elections
domains.notes: Foundation domain. SAVE Act Senate failure, Callais VRA evisceration, ballot initiative wave all documented. April/May 2026 current.

domains.title: Institutional Integrity / Civil Service and Executive Accountability
domains.notes: Foundation domain. Covered in proposal body; standalone research in domain-civil-service-resilience.md. Schedule Policy/Career, DOGE, IG gutting all documented.

domains.title: Democratic Participation
domains.notes: Includes baby bonds, housing rights, civic infrastructure. Deep proposal section only.

domains.title: Digital Government Infrastructure
domains.notes: Estonia X-Road model; DOGE data weaponization; digital exclusion. Proposal body only.

domains.title: Fiscal Reform
domains.notes: $688B tax gap; wealth concentration as democratic threat; OECD Pillar Two. Proposal body only.

domains.title: Judicial Independence and Reform
domains.notes: Foundation domain. Humphrey's Executor collapse, Wilcox shadow-docket, Slaughter June 2026 decision. Trump v. Wilcox; Loper Bright. April/May 2026 current.

domains.title: Rights Protection and Civil Liberties
domains.notes: Campaign finance, anti-protest laws, DISCLOSE Act. Proposal body only.

domains.title: Media and Information Ecosystem
domains.notes: Local news collapse, algorithmic amplification. Proposal body only; see domain-media-freedom-recovery.md for standalone research.

domains.title: Federalism and Local Democracy
domains.notes: Preemption laws; state rights floors; 94 restrictive voting laws. Proposal body only.

domains.title: Education
domains.notes: K-12 equity, universal pre-K, civics. Proposal body only.

domains.title: Healthcare Structural Reform
domains.notes: Proposal body. See Domain 31 (OBBBA/Medicaid) and Domain 31x for current crisis research.

domains.title: Infrastructure
domains.notes: Proposal body only.

domains.title: Housing
domains.notes: Proposal body only. Also carries Constitutional Amendments numbering in some cross-references — see cross-ref notes.

domains.title: Criminal Justice and Policing
domains.notes: Brady violations, prosecutorial ethics, use-of-force standards. Proposal body only.

domains.title: Environment and Climate
domains.notes: Proposal body only.

domains.title: Immigration and Citizenship
domains.notes: ICE algorithmic tools (FALCON, ImmigrationOS) documented in Domain 36. Proposal body only.

domains.title: Labor and Employment
domains.notes: NLRB quorum gap (Humphrey's Executor collapse) creates critical link to D6. Proposal body only.

domains.title: Social Safety Net
domains.notes: Proposal body. OBBBA $911B Medicaid cut documented in Domain 31 standalone.

domains.title: National Security and Foreign Policy
domains.notes: General foreign policy framework in proposal; current war powers crisis documented in D19f and D28 standalones.

domains.title: War Powers Reform — Iran Operation
domains.notes: Largest domain in corpus (29,600 words). Operation Epic Fury; May 1 deadline non-compliance fully documented. May 2026 current.

domains.title: Economic Concentration and Antitrust
domains.notes: Proposal body only.

domains.title: Data Privacy and Digital Surveillance
domains.notes: Proposal body; FISA 702 detailed in Domain 25 standalone.

domains.title: Reparations and Racial Justice
domains.notes: Proposal body only. HR 40 analysis; racial wealth gap; Callais VRA link to D1.

domains.title: Trade Policy, Tariff Unilateralism, and Economic Sovereignty
domains.notes: IEEPA struck down Feb 2026; Section 122 litigation; Section 301 replacement strategy. SCOTUS Learning Resources. April 2026 current.

domains.title: FISA Section 702 — April 2026 Reauthorization Outcome
domains.notes: 45-day extension signed April 30; June 12 deadline. Three-year warrantless renewal failed; CBDC ban stripped. May 2026 current.

domains.title: Government Accountability and Institutional Resilience
domains.notes: IG firings; $18/dollar IG ROI; ballot initiative nullification; Skrmetti. April 2026 current.

domains.title: Higher Education and Academic Freedom
domains.notes: Harvard $2.2B freeze; Khalil/Öztürk/Mahdawi visa revocations; Hungary model endorsed by Vance. April 2026 current.

domains.title: War Powers and Venezuela Military Unilateralism
domains.notes: Operation Absolute Resolve; 'arrest operation' loophole; five Senate war powers vote defeats. Two versions exist in /domains/; both production-ready.

domains.title: Prosecutorial Weaponization and DOJ Capture
domains.notes: Foundation domain (enforcement cannot be assumed). SPLC indictment; 22-case pattern; Abrego Garcia vindictive prosecution. April 29 2026 current.

domains.title: Healthcare Access Collapse — OBBBA and Medicaid Crisis
domains.notes: 11.8M disenrollment; Arkansas 2018 precedent; NVRA-Medicaid voter registration nexus. January 2027 work requirements effective.

domains.title: Healthcare Tariff Collision — Pharmaceutical Supply Shock
domains.notes: Scope document. Strait of Hormuz compound threat; MFN pricing strategy; electoral political economy of tariff-healthcare collision.

domains.title: State Legislative Autocratization
domains.notes: REDMAP 2.0; North Carolina dark money court capture; 295+ ballot initiative suppression bills; Hungary model. 2026 SOS races as immediate test.

domains.title: Congressional Power-of-the-Purse Fiscal Authority Reassertion
domains.notes: Foundation domain (fiscal enforcement gap). $425B+ withheld appropriations; Category C expansion; GAO defunding threat; post-Loper vectors.

domains.title: Supreme Court OT2026 Term Preview and Post-Loper Landscape
domains.notes: Foundation domain (doctrinal landscape for all litigation-based reform). Loper Bright trifecta; Slaughter June 2026; presidential immunity; universal injunction elimination.

domains.title: AI Governance, Algorithmic Accountability, and Democratic Authority
domains.notes: WISeR; Palantir/ICE; SSA disability AI; DOGE terminations; post-Loper vulnerability. Six statutory reforms. April 2026 current.

domains.title: Federal Executive Interference in the 2026 Midterm Elections
domains.notes: DOJ voter roll litigation (23 cases); draft emergency EO; ICE-at-polls threat; CISA $700M cut; Hungary April 2026 counterfactual. April 2026 current.

domains.title: Post-Election Section 3 Litigation and Congressional Certification Recovery
domains.notes: Scope document. Article I Section 5 certification; Fulton County FBI raid precedent; January 3, 2027 certification window.

domains.title: State Election Security Coordination — Post-CISA Architecture
domains.notes: Scope document. CISA EI-ISAC eliminated; MS-ISAC cooperative agreement defunded; state-level security vacuum. 6-month window before November 2026.

domains.title: Foreign and Transnational Interference in US Democratic Institutions
domains.notes: Russia/China/Iran operational surges; FARA enforcement collapse; Storm-1516 near-daily output; independent FARA unit reform. April 2026 current.

domains.title: Financial Sector Independence and Banking System Resilience
domains.notes: Fed Chair Powell term expires May 15, 2026; Trump v. Cook pending; Humphrey's Executor collapse threatens Fed removal protections. DOGE Treasury payment system infiltration.

domains.title: Civil Service Resilience and Protection
domains.notes: Structural mechanisms for surviving politicization; MSPB enforcement gap; Schedule Policy/Career analysis; independent Civil Service Commission design.

domains.title: Foreign and Transnational Interference (standalone version)
domains.notes: Full Phase 3 research document (distinct from D37-FT which is the domain folder version). Both document the same subject with some variation in depth and framing.

domains.title: Information Access, FOIA, and Investigative Capacity
domains.notes: Characterized as 'prerequisite for every other domain' in the document itself. FOIA dismantlement; DOGE records opacity; IG firings' information effect; WPA protections.

domains.title: Judicial Independence Recovery — Structural Mechanisms
domains.notes: Recovery pathway post-capture. SCOTUS term limits; judicial ethics enforcement; assignment reforms; 8-week Slaughter advocacy window.

domains.title: Congressional Institutional Independence and Legislative Capacity
domains.notes: OTA elimination; staffing collapse; GAO defunding; reconciliation Byrd Rule manipulation; 119th Congress procedural breakdown.

domains.title: Media Freedom and Journalistic Protection
domains.notes: PRESS Act stalled; journalist visa revocations; AP/Reuters Oval Office ban; self-censorship market dynamics. Frame: press is prerequisite infrastructure for D1 and D37.

domains.title: Democratic Renewal Proposal — Implementation Roadmap
domains.notes: Strategic implementation sequencing. Three recovery waves. Prerequisites: all six foundation domains. Should be read last by institutional recipients.

domains.title: Phase 3 Candidate 5: Finance and Fiscal Architecture
domains.notes: IRS enforcement collapse; OMB apportionment manipulation; post-Loper Treasury/IRS vulnerability; independent fiscal institution design.

domains.title: Phase 3 Candidate 6: Democratic Participation and Election Security
domains.notes: Paper ballot + RLA security-participation synergy; automatic voter registration; distributed administration with national coordination.

domains.title: Phase 3 Candidate 7: Technology Governance and Digital Rights
domains.notes: AI/surveillance/cryptography governance trilemma; innovation-security-rights framework; post-Loper executive-order-only framework vulnerability.

domains.title: Phase 3 Candidate 8: Civil Service Hiring and Protections Post-Capture
domains.notes: Meritocratic hiring protection reform; post-Schedule F protections; independent commission design. Complements D-CivSvc.

edges.note: Gerrymandering is the primary mechanism suppressing electoral reform passage
Voting rights infrastructure and election interference operate on the same terrain
VRA litigation depends on judicial independence; Callais VRA evisceration in D6
Watson v. RNC and Callais both in D35 cert/decision pipeline
Medicaid enrollment as NVRA voter registration site; disenrollment suppresses registration
Civil service dismantlement and judicial capture are simultaneous capture vectors
IG system is civil service accountability infrastructure
D-CivSvc provides the structural recovery mechanisms D2 proposes at high level
DOJ capture enables judicial erosion; judicial independence requires independent enforcement
D35 documents the doctrinal landscape that D6's reform proposals must navigate
D-JIR provides the recovery pathway for the capture mechanisms D6 documents
Iran and Venezuela are two flanks of the same WPR constitutional failure
Congressional fiscal authority is the enforcement mechanism for war powers constraints
Pharmaceutical tariffs create the supply shock documented in D31x
FISA warrantless query authority creates election organizer surveillance window
D34 fiscal enforcement is the material mechanism behind D26's accountability reforms
Venezuela operation unauthorized spending is a power-of-the-purse failure, not just war powers
DOJ capture enables midterm interference; D29 establishes that enforcement cannot be assumed
D31x is the OBBBA-tariff collision analysis that builds on D31's base Medicaid architecture
State autocratization creates the infrastructure for federal midterm interference
Legislative capacity is the prerequisite for meaningful fiscal oversight
Post-Loper landscape determines which AI governance mechanisms are legally viable
Humphrey's Executor collapse via D35 determines Fed removal protection durability
AI-driven election administration tools are the technology dimension of election security gap
D37a is the post-election continuation of D37's pre-election interference analysis
D37b fills the state-level security vacuum created by CISA dismantlement documented in D37
Foreign interference amplifies and operates through the domestic infrastructure gaps in D37
D29 litigation cannot proceed without FOIA records establishing political direction
D34 fiscal enforcement requires GAO access to apportionment data
Informed electorate for D1 voting rights requires press infrastructure that D-MediaFreedom documents
Press targeting is a midterm interference mechanism; suppressing journalism suppresses exposure

circular_dependencies.description: Judicial independence requires independent DOJ enforcement; DOJ capture enables judicial erosion. Neither is upstream of the other.
Congressional fiscal authority enforces war powers constraints; war powers violations normalize executive fiscal unilateralism.
Electoral reform is blocked by gerrymandering; gerrymandering is an electoral reform problem.
